vulnerability.title: Disable Core Dumps for All Users
vulnerability.description: To disable core dumps for all users, add the following line to /etc/security/limits.conf, or to a file within the /etc/security/limits.d/ directory: * hard core 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q pam; }; then SECURITY_LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.conf" DROPIN_DIR="/etc/security/limits.d" DROPIN_FILE="$DROPIN_DIR/10-ssg-hardening.conf" REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE="^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+0\s*$" # Remove bad configuration in drop-ins if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then for override in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do if [ -f "$override" ] && ! grep -qE "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$override"; then sed -ir -E '/^[[:space:]]*\*[[:space:]]+hard[[:space:]]+core[[:space:]]+/ s/^/#/' "$override" fi done fi if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ] && grep -qEr "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$DROPIN_DIR"; then exit 0 elif [ ! -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ] && grep -qE "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$SECURITY_LIMITS_FILE"; then exit 0 else mkdir -p "$DROP

triage_decision.reason: Adding a PAM limits configuration to disable core dumps is a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services. It is a simple text edit with no risk of lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; core dumps may contain sensitive data

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need plan. Plan: modify /etc/security/limits.conf or add file in /etc/security/limits.d. Use sed to add '* hard core 0'. Ensure not duplicate. Also remove any bad config lines. Approach: read /etc/security/limits.d; if exists, choose 10-ssg-hardening.conf. If not, create. But as per rule, to modify file, readable, use read_file. We need to check if file contains line already matching pattern, if so skip adding. If not, append.

Let's propose to create /etc/security/limits.d/10-ssg-hardening.conf if not exist. Add line. Also ensure * hard core 0 not commented. Use sed -i to uncomment if commented.

Also could modify main file. Plan: check /etc/security/limits.conf, if no line, add. But better to add dropin. Provide plan. Then run review_plan.
